name: La Jolla
shortIntro: Cliffs, coves, and a village core—coastal San Diego at its most refined.

name: Del Mar
shortIntro: Small beach town feel with seasonal energy—quiet most days, lively when the races are in.

name: Coronado
shortIntro: Island-town rhythm with bikes, beaches, and an easy routine right across from Downtown.

name: Encinitas
shortIntro: Surf-town energy with a grown-up routine—coastal, laid-back, and community-driven.

name: Solana Beach
shortIntro: Tight coastal town between Del Mar and Encinitas—Cedros, Fletcher Cove, surf-and-design energy without big-city pace.

name: Rancho Santa Fe
shortIntro: Estate living with space, privacy, and a country-club pace—close to the coast, but tucked away.

name: Carmel Valley
shortIntro: School-and-park centered North County suburb with easy routines and commuter convenience.

name: North Park
shortIntro: Walkable city neighborhood with patios, breweries, and real sidewalk life.

name: Downtown San Diego
shortIntro: High-rise, waterfront, and event energy—city living with views and building amenities.

name: Mission Valley
shortIntro: Central hub for commuting and errands—more base camp than village neighborhood.

name: Clairemont
shortIntro: Central, residential, and underrated—mid-century homes and easy access to the whole county.

name: Bay Park
shortIntro: Low-key coastal pocket near Mission Bay—calm streets and a great home base feel.

name: Point Loma
shortIntro: Harbor life and older-home character—local coastal San Diego with its own rhythm.

name: Pacific Beach
shortIntro: Classic beach neighborhood energy—walkable pockets, ocean days, and a more social pace.

name: Scripps Ranch
shortIntro: Established inland neighborhood with parks, trails, and a calm day-to-day.

name: La Mesa
shortIntro: Small-town village feel in East County—walkable core, calm streets, and a steady pace.

name: Chula Vista
shortIntro: A full-size South Bay city with real neighborhoods—space, variety, and a grounded feel.

name: Eastlake
shortIntro: Planned South Bay communities—parks, schools, and an easy suburban routine.

name: Otay
shortIntro: Otay Ranch + Otay Mesa—newer housing pockets and border-adjacent commerce with a practical vibe.

name: Imperial Beach
shortIntro: Laid-back coastal South Bay with a local feel—more quiet beach town than destination.

name: National City
shortIntro: Working South Bay city with strong access and more approachable pricing—micro-location matters.

name: El Cajon
shortIntro: Inland value with more space—warmer weather, car-first living, and street-by-street variety.
